symbols: domed church — the vault of heaven brought to earth in sacred architecture
ancient stone walls — the endurance of the Armenian Christian faith through centuries of persecution
hilltop setting — the city set on a hill that cannot be hidden, a beacon of faith
description: A small, warmly colored painting depicting an ancient Armenian stone church on a hillside. The building features a prominent central dome with a golden or ochre-colored roof, thick stone walls in warm sandy and brown tones, narrow arched windows, and a smaller bell tower or belfry to the right. An arched entrance or ruined gateway stands at the far right. The surrounding terrain is dry and grassy with patches of green. The sky is a clear, soft blue with light clouds. The brushwork is loose and expressive, with warm earth tones dominating the composition.
